entry.title: SLD DB Export/ Import issue
entry.content:
We have an issue where the system admin attempts to do an export for the DB, but by mistake he does import, and the database is being overwritten to the last successful backup

Is there a way to create a user on the SLD and limit his permissions to the export operation rather than import? or is there any way to avoid this? woraround? tool to be added to the SLD? anything

entry.title: SAP Commerce - Custom Labels Overwritten When Running "Localize Types" During Update
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m facing an issue with SAP Commerce during system updates, specifically when using migration data and running an update running system.

I noticed that some labels are being overwritten by standard SAP Commerce values. To address this, I added my custom labels in the createEssentialData method inside initialDataSystemSetup.

Here’s the behavior I’m seeing:

  • If I go to HAC → Update and select only Create Essential Data, everything works fine and my custom labels are correctly applied.
  • However, if I also select Localize Types, my custom labels get overwritten again by the default ones.

So my question is:
Why does the "Localize Types" option override labels defined in createEssentialData?
Is there a recommended way to persist custom labels so they are not reset during localization updates?

Any insights or best practices would be really appreciated.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting - New Balance Validation Process
entry.content:
Hi Andreas,

thanks for your feedback!

With regards to your question on the validation result analysis (or corresponding CDS views), there is currently no concrete plan to build this. The feature can be requested on our customer influence platform (CIP).

Best,

Florian

entry.title: Re: Relationship between CBC tenant and S/4HANA Cloud Pub...
entry.content:
Hi @byd ,

Let's change a perspective to look at your questions.

The CBC is a central configuration tool, not only for the Public Cloud, but eventually for more SAP solutions. So it is one CBC for many solutions.

Within the CBC, you work on workspaces/projects, which is targeting to one specific configuration or development tenant in the Public Cloud. So it is 1:1 relationship between a workspace and a tenant.

In the 3SL, only the development system (configuration tenant 100 and development tenant 080) can be targeted/connected by the CBC.  The Test and Production tenants only receive transports from the development system. So they don't connect to the CBC directly. 

In the meantime, if you use Parallel Line, you can connect its configuration tenant 120 to a new workspace in the CBC as well.

At the same time, the CBC can have multiple workspaces without a target system, I call them dummy workspaces. You cannot deploy its content to anywhere.

Hope this helps you to understand the relationship between them.

Best,

George

entry.title: Third-Party Payments with SIPO
entry.content:
This blog post introduces business scenarios and explains how to process payment with SIPO in Czech Republic for SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition. 

SIPO (Soustředěné Inkaso Plateb Obyvatelstva) that is used for direct debit of multiple payments through the Czech Post’s system. It is a national payment aggregation system operated by the Czech Post. This business concept relates to payments through a third party. 

Business Scenarios

Let’s start with the parties involved in SIPO payments.  

  1. Customers that have SIPO connection number pay on a regular basis 
  2. Service providers that offer services of utilities, multimedia, or internet, send payment requests to SIPO 
  3. Czech Post Office, or the SIPO operator, collects all payment requests, aggregates them into one payment, and distribute paid money by customers to service providers 
  4. Banks that serve as payment channels carry out direct debit from bank accounts

There are two business scenarios of payments involving SIPO: 

Scenario 1: You, as a service provider, post receivable items. After executing a payment run, you send a SIPO file, as an outgoing file, to the Czech Post for informing the customers to pay.

Scenario 2: Continuing with scenario 1, you, as a service provider, want to clear the open items by processing incoming SIPO files after your customer completes the payment. 

Prerequisites

Bank Account Settings

Since the payments with SIPO order are not directly paid out from the payer's bank account, instead it is paid out from the post office, it is necessary to create a fictive bank account and account ID for the post office.

Open the Manage Bank Accounts app.  
For your company code, create a fictive Bank Account and Account ID for the Czech Post. 

Define Parameters for Paying Out

Since payment is carried out through the third party (Czech Post),  you need to identify which payee whom the post office pays.   

Use the Define Parameters for Foreign Payment Transactions view (TFK012D) in the transaction SM30 to fill the service provider's SIPO number in the Customer number field.

Define Payment Methods 

Use the Define Payment Methods (ID: 101952) View cluster VC_TFK042Z in the transaction SM34. 

Create a payment method or use the pre-delivered format - CZ_PSTLPAYMENT_SIPO as the payment medium format for Czech SIPO payments.

Define Payment Lot Specifications

Use the Define Payment Lot Specification view ( V_TFKIDEXPC_ZK ) in the transaction SM30. 

Select the format type Czech Republic: Postal Payment Item (SIPO file) (CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-ZA) and complete the Bank statement settings section.  
Note: It is not required to configure the Czech Republic: Postal Payment Header (SIPO file) (CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-PZ). 

Define Interpretation Rules for Note to Payee

Use  the Define Interpretation Rules for Note to Payee view cluster (VC_TFK006) to assign keywords.

Procedure

  1. You post contract accounting receivable items. 
  2. Execute a payment run. The payment order then is created. 
  3. During the payment run, the system uses the payment format CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-SIPO to generate the payment file for the Czech Post. 
  4. You then send the payment file to Czech Post. 
    Czech Post informs the customer to conduct the payment.

Processing SIPO Incoming Payment Files 

After the customer pays, the Czech Post sends back the SIPO files.  

The SIPO files contain the following files:

  • SIPO header file – Includes the total settlement amount that matches the bank statement of the incoming payment 
  • SIPO item file – lists the clearing of open receivable items per customer’s individual SIPO number 

You have to import both files in the Manage Incoming Payment File (3rd-Party Payments) app (F1680).

  1. Open the Manage Incoming Payment File (3rd-Party Payments) app (F1680). 
    • Select Import and choose Third-party Payment.
    • Select Czech Republic: Postal Payment Header (SIPO file) and upload the SIPO header file.
    • Repeat the same operation and upload the Czech Republic: Postal Payment Item (SIPO file).

  2. Open the Process Imported Third-Party Payments app (F9000) to schedule a run. 
    • Select Third-Party Payment: Transfer data as the job template.  
    • In the Parameters, select Czech Republic: Postal Payment Item (SIPO file) (CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-ZA) as a format type.  
      Note: When processing the imported file, you only need to process the item files. (Header files are not required.)  
    • Schedule a run and you can see that the payment lot number is created in the log.

      If you select the Post Lot check box and you can directly process the payment order lot without additional operation.

  3. Use the payment lot and process it in the Process Payment (Order) Lot transaction (FP45) and clear open items of the customer. If you did not select the checkbox in the previous step.

Is this blog post useful to you? Leave your comments below to let us know your thoughts.

entry.title: S/4HANA: “Track Sales Order” app is showing incorrect status
entry.content:
1. For cancelled sales order, in track sales order app we have below status in overall fulfillment: “Not yet processed” This status is not giving indication that sales order is cancel and it is giving indication that order is still open, please check if status for this kind of order can be marked as “Completely Processed”.
  2. For order where few line item rejected and other are complete order is showing overall fulfillment as partially processed, it should be completely processed.

I have tried the sap note 3136916 - Rejected sales order items remain open - SAP for Me  but suggested configuration is not working as expected. 

May I seek experts help to fix this issue?

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud v2: Enhanced ES Service CQC Deployment Readiness
entry.content:
Introduction

We're excited to announce significant enhancements to our Enterprise Support (ES) Continuous Quality Check (CQC) for Deployment Readiness service for SAP Commerce Cloud v2. These improvements strengthen our commitment to ensuring successful deployments by adding critical cluster configuration validations and enhanced cutover planning capabilities. This enhanced service provides customers with greater confidence in their deployment readiness while focusing specifically on pre-go-live optimization to minimize risks and ensure operational excellence.

What's New in CQC Deployment Readiness

Enhanced Cluster Configuration Checks

Our enhanced CQC service now includes comprehensive validation of cluster configurations, with a particular focus on ensuring TaskEngine is properly disabled on Storefront Nodes,[2]. This critical check prevents performance bottlenecks and ensures optimal resource allocation across your SAP Commerce Cloud infrastructure. By validating this configuration early in the deployment process, we help prevent common issues that could impact storefront performance and customer experience during high-traffic periods.

The technical benefits include improved system stability, optimized resource utilization, and reduced risk of performance degradation during peak operations. This validation ensures your cluster architecture follows SAP best practices for production environments.

Improved Cutover Planning Verification

The enhanced service introduces robust verification mechanisms to confirm successful deployment completion[2]. These new capabilities include comprehensive validation of all deployment components, and confirmation that all services are functioning correctly post-deployment.

This systematic approach to cutover verification ensures smoother go-live processes by identifying and addressing potential issues before they impact production operations.

Focus Shift: Pre-Go-Live Optimization

Updated CQC Scope

Our CQC service has been strategically refocused to prioritize pre-go-live checks, moving away from post-implementation reviews to proactive deployment readiness validation. This shift ensures that potential issues are identified and resolved before they can impact on your production environment.

The updated approach emphasizes preventive quality assurance rather than reactive problem-solving, and reduces deployment risks and accelerates time-to-production.

Key Benefits for Customers

Risk Reduction: By conducting comprehensive pre-go-live validations, we significantly reduce the likelihood of deployment failures and production issues. The enhanced checks identify configuration problems before they impact your business operations.

Deployment Confidence: The improved verification processes provide development and operations teams with greater confidence in deployment success. Detailed validation reports offer clear insights into system readiness and highlight any areas requiring attention.

Operational Efficiency: The focus on pre-go-live optimization streamlines the deployment process and reduces the need for post-deployment troubleshooting. This approach minimizes downtime and accelerates the path to productive system operation.

Next Steps

Immediate Actions: Customers planning SAP Commerce Cloud v2 deployments should schedule their enhanced CQC service at least six weeks before their planned go-live date to allow adequate time for implementing recommendations.

Service Booking: Request the enhanced CQC service through the "Get Support" application in SAP for Me, selecting "Continuous Quality Check & Improvement Services" as the product and "Service request for a Cloud Solution" as the product function.

Timeline: The enhanced CQC service is available immediately for all SAP Commerce Cloud v2 implementations. SAP recommends booking the service four weeks before your go-live date to ensure sufficient time for review and implementation of recommendations.

Support Resources: For assistance with service requests or questions about the enhanced CQC capabilities, contact your local Customer Interaction Center (CIC) or reach out to the SAP Enterprise Support team.

Ready to ensure your SAP Commerce Cloud v2 deployment success?

Visit: Continuous quality check (CQC) and improvement services

entry.title: Re: incrementalMigrationJobNonLP failing intermittently w...
entry.content:
I suggest you to check how the DB Sync toolkit is executing the transformation scripts. The error suggests that the script is attempting to recreate the table MIGRATIONTOOLKIT_TF_TYPEINFO, which already exists. 

( There is already an object named 'MIGRATIONTOOLKIT_TF_TYPEINFO' in the database.) 

As a suggestion, you can verify:

whether the incremental job is running in parallel
whether initialization logic is being triggered more than once
whether any process is resetting or affecting MIGRATIONTOOLKIT_* tables

Please note: sap-commerce-db-sync - This repository is provided "as-is"; SAP does not provide any additional support.

SAP Services team is the creator and main contributor to SAP Commerce DB Sync, the team can provide assistance as a paid engagement. If you plan to setup SAP Commerce DB Sync for your project, we highly recommend involving SAP Services to ensure a successful implementation.

For more details, please refer to:-  - 2706322 - What is Support – What is Consulting: Cloud Solutions

entry.title: Re: Replicate terminated utility contracts - CDP - Utilities Accelerator
entry.content:
Hi Atul_Joshi85,

Thank you for your reply! Our CDP needs the full contract details, e.g. the tariff type as well.

We opened a ticket and SAP has now released note 3720091, saying that the correction is available.

A new DRF Outbound Parameter was introduced to select the timeframe for inactive contracts to be replicated. The necessary customizing is described in the note.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Error in MD03 MRP run "in house production time i...
entry.content:
Why Case 1 vs. Case 2 behave differently is because

  • in Case 2 (Material 0022840001 / Plant 1173), the system is actively validating the processing time vs. base quantity relationship in the Work Scheduling view and throwing the “in‑house production time too long” error.
  • in Case 1 (Material 021475‑000 / Plant 1306) even if processing time > base quantity, the system does not throw the error. This usually points to different plant‑specific customizing or master data defaults.

So the recommended approach is to keep the earlier checks (Work Scheduling view values, routing consistency, plant‑specific settings). Additionally, compare MRP group, scheduling margin key, lot size procedure, and production scheduling profile between the two cases. The difference in these settings is most likely why Case 1 tolerates high processing time vs. base quantity, while Case 2 does not.

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
entry.content: Is this to be done multiple times , like for example DEV , QA , PRD or the above steps need to be done in PRD only ?

entry.title: Re: How is Column Store fetch faster than Row Store?
entry.content: This is a very clear and practical breakdown of why columnar storage fundamentally changes the economics of data access. The use of real byte‑level calculations makes the trade‑offs tangible, especially for teams who still assume indexing can compensate for row‑store limitations at scale. What stands out is how you highlight the hidden costs—random I/O, cache misses, and unnecessary row materialization—that developers often overlook. The comparison also reinforces why analytical workloads benefit so much from sequential scans, compression, and vectorized execution. It’s a great reminder that storage format isn’t just a physical detail; it directly shapes query performance, CPU efficiency, and system scalability. Posts like this help bridge the gap between database theory and real‑world engineering decisions.

entry.title: Cannot access IAS Admin Console – redirected to /admin/forbidden.html (BTP Trial)
entry.content:
I have a SAP BTP trial subaccount with Cloud Identity Services subscribed.

 

I can access the BTP cockpit and other services (Destinations, SAP Build Work Zone, etc.),
but when trying to access the IAS Administration Console I am always redirected to:
/admin/forbidden.html

 

I am the only user of the tenant and it seems the Administrator role was lost.
There is no option to "Log on with a different Identity Provider".

 


IAS tenant: awbofyup0.trial-accounts.ondemand.com

 

Could SAP please restore an IAS Administrator for this tenant? I could provide whoever is necesary with my subacount name and my user.

entry.title: How to Use Custom Fields in Event-Based Settlement for Assets Under Construction
entry.content:
Co-author: H. Trefftz

Learn how custom fields can be used in the event-based settlement process from cost posting through to final asset reporting.

Since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508, you can use event-based settlement to settle investment projects to assets under construction.

Release 2602 takes this further by introducing custom field support throughout the entire settlement process. You can use app extensibility and define custom fields that you can then use throughout the whole event-based settlement process: You can post to the custom fields in the cost posting, for example, when posting an invoice. The custom fields are also posted to in the settlement postings: The custom field values are inherited when the WBS element is settled to the asset under construction (AuC) and also when the AuC is settled to the completed asset. Finally, the customer fields are also available in reporting. This means better tracking and more meaningful reporting in both General Ledger Accounting and Asset Accounting.

Using an example, you are guided through the entire process - from the definition of custom fields to reporting in Asset Accounting. This example additionally contains the substitution of a custom field with another field.

Step 1: Preparation - Define and Enable Custom Fields

In our example, we want to create two custom fields for the following purposes:

Custom Field,
Name

Custom Field,
Technical Name

Purpose

AUC Assignment

YY1_AUC_COBL

Have an input field for user input in the following apps:

  • Post General Journal Entries (F0718) and
  • Manage Supplier Invoice (F0859)

AUC User Plant

YY1_AUC_JE_ITEM

During the posting to the WBS element of an investment project, we want to transfer the content of the Plant field to this custom field. As a result, we have the information about the original plant available throughout the whole process and see its value in the settlement journal entry.

We define our custom fields in the Custom Fields app.

When defining the custom fields, we use the following business contexts:

  • Accounting: Coding Block (FINS_CODING_BLOCK) and
  • Accounting: Journal Entry Item (FINS_JOURNAL_ENTRY_ITEM)

After we have published the custom fields, the list of custom fields looks as shown in Figure 1.

Figure 1: List of custom fields with their business contexts (Custom Fields app)

We then need to enable the field for usage in our apps. We do this on the User Interfaces tab of the Custom Fields app.

  • To have the custom fields available in the posting apps and in the substitution app, the corresponding OData services need to be enabled.
  • To make the custom fields available in the reports of the Asset Transactions app, we at least need to enable the fields for the OData Service FAA_ASSET_TRANSACTIONS_SRV.

In our example, we enable our first custom field YY1_AUC_COBL for the input in both posting apps and the asset transactional reporting. This is shown in Figures 2a-d.

Figure 2a: Enabling custom field YY1_AUC_COBL for the Manage Journal Entries app

Figure 2b: Enabling custom field YY1_AUC_COBL for the apps Post General Journal Entries, Display Line Items in General Ledger, and the Journal CDS view

Figure 2c: Enabling custom field YY1_AUC_COBL for the app Manage Supplier Invoice

Figure 2d: Enabling custom field YY1_AUC_COBL for the Asset Transactions app

Furthermore, we enable our second custom field (YY1_AUC_JE_ITEM) for substitution and for reporting in the Asset Transactions app:

Figure 3a: Enabling custom field YY1_AUC_JE_ITEM for the app Manage Substitution and Validation Rules (F4406), Variant Journal Entries

Figure 3b: Same as figure 2d. - Enabling custom field YY1_AUC_JE_ITEM for the Asset Transactions app (F1614)

If we wanted to use the custom fields in further apps, we would have to enable the corresponding services, too.

Step 2: Preparation - Define Substitution Rule for Custom Field

We use the app Manage Substitution and Validation Rules (F4406), variant Journal Entries, to define the substitution for our second custom field YY1_AUC_JE_ITEM. During a cost posting to the WBS element, the system shall replace the custom field YY1_AUC_JE_ITEM with the Plant entry.

Figure 4a: Creating the substitution rule, entry screen

 

Figure 4b: Creating the substitution rule, detail screen

After we had created the substitution rule, we have activated it.

Step 3: Preparation - Create Investment Project and AUC for Event-Based Settlement

A final preparation step is still missing before we can use the custom fields in the settlement process: We create an investment project with a WBS element, assigning an event-based settlement profile.

We create the investment project IM_EB1 in the app Project Control - Enterprise Projects (F3215), assign the settlement profile EB0001, and then release the project.

Figure 5: Creating the investment project (in app Project Control - Enterprise Projects (F3215))

The system automatically creates a corresponding asset under construction (AuC).

We then create a settlement rule for final settlement from the AuC to the completed asset (1010-20000000-0).

Step 4: Posting - Make Postings with Custom Field Input

Substep 4-1: Post General Journal Entry

Since we have enabled the custom field YY1_AUC_COBL for the required OData services, we can enter values for this custom field in the posting apps when posting costs to the WBS element.

In our example, we make a posting in the app Post General Journal Entries (F0718) where we enter the following account assignments:

  • WBS element: IM_EB1
  • Custom field AUC Assignment (YY1_AUC_COBL): Input 1

Figure 6: Posting in app Post General Journal Entries (F0718)

Substep 4-2: Post Supplier Invoice

We make a second posting in the Manage Supplier Invoice app (F0859) where we enter the following account assignments:

  • WBS element: IM_EB1
  • Custom field AUC Assignment (YY1_AUC_COBL): Input 2

Figure 7: Posting in app Manage Supplier Invoice (F0859)

Substep 4-3: Post Purchase Order with 'Plant' Substitution and Subsequent Goods Receipt

We create a purchase order for our investment project (WBS element).

We do this in the app Manage Purchase Orders (F0842A), where we specify the following:

  • Account Assignment Category: Project (P)
  • Plant: New Plant FIO1 (FIO1)
  • WBS Element: IM_EB1

Figure 8: Creating a purchase order (in app Manage Purchase Orders (F0842A))

Later, we post the goods receipt that belongs to the purchase order. We do this in the app Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document (F0843); we specify the purchase order.

Figure 9: Posting the goods receipt (in app Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document (F0843))

The cost posting is then made to the WBS element IM_EB1. Since we have created the investment project (with WBS element IM_EB1) with an event-based settlement profile, the costs are settled directly to the AuC. The process flow is shown in figure 10.

Figure 10: Process flow from purchase order to AuC

As defined in the substitution rule, our second custom field YY1_AUC_JE_ITEM is replaced with the plant entry of the goods receipt journal entry (5000000001) and the custom field is also copied to the settlement journal entry (100000003).

Figure 11: Line items for the goods receipt journal entry (in app Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document (F0843))

Figure 12: Line items for the settlement journal entry (in app Manage Journal Entries (F0717A)

Step 5: Posting - Post Final Settlement to Completed Asset

During the settlement of the AuC to the final receiver asset (completed asset) 20000000-0, all custom field information is taken over to the line items for the AuC retirement posting on the one hand and to the line items for the asset acquisition (for the completed asset) on the other.

Figure 13: Posting of final settlement from AuC to completed asset

Step 6: Reporting

For reporting purposes, you can use the Asset Transaction app (F1614). In its view Journal Entry Tracing for Settlement, you can display the settlement values for both, for the AuC and for the completed asset including the custom field account assignments.

Figure 14: Reporting the settlement postings including the custom field account assignments (app Asset Transaction (F1614), view Journal Entry Tracing for Settlement)

Conclusion and More Information

Custom fields flow seamlessly through the entire settlement process, while data consistency is ensured. Whether you're tracking specific cost categories, plant assignments, or other custom-specific data, custom fields make it possible.

The setup of custom fields requires enabling OData services and may involve creating substitution rules, but once configured, the process runs smoothly and automatically.

Want to learn more about event-based settlement? - For more information, see the help portal documentation under Assets Under Construction: Management and Settlement, Event-Based Settlement | SAP Help Portal and Extensibility: Using Custom Fields with Event-Based Settlement

For general information about the enablement of custom fields in the accounting business contexts as well as for UI adaptions, see SAP Note 3448139.

entry.title: Re: Limitation - Change in Availability of Dependents Inf...
entry.content:
@SDaldini This is only for customers who are going to enable CSF dependents from EC. If they don't enable CSF dependents the it will work as before..

CSF dependents is rolled out by EC from 1H 2026, for Greenfield customers or customer who do not have existing dependents data..

Existing customers who r using dependents won't be able to enable CSF dependents in this release.

https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-employee-central/implementing-dependents/setting-up-dependents

Thanks,

Gopi

entry.title: Re: Recording and slide deck of SAP on IBM i April webcast available for download
entry.content: I don't think it can be overstated how important this webcast is for SAP on IBM i (and any other IBM i) clients.  With a few simple SQL queries, you may find that your system is secure...or more likely far less secure than you thought.  It is well worth the time to download, review and check your systems.

entry.title: Re: Automated bank reconciliations hana sap
entry.content: Hi Tulsi, can I receive access to see the informations? Thank you, Camelia

entry.title: Issue with Bank Statement Processing Rules for Bank Fees
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently experiencing an issue with the processing rules configured for bank fees in bank statement uploads.

We have defined an automated rule for our client to enable automatic posting of bank fees during the bank statement import. The rule is set up with the following criteria:

  • Company Code: equal to 4400
  • Memo Line: contains (chinese characters)
  • House Bank: between BOC1 and BOC2
  • House Bank Account: between CNY1 and CNY2
  • House Bank Account: between EUR1 and EUR2
  • House Bank Account: between USD1 and USD2

The posting is configured to GL account 71000000 along with the relevant cost center.

However, we have observed that when the memo line contains the text "BANK CHARGES" (Latin characters), the system correctly posts the bank fees automatically. In contrast, when Chinese characters are used, the automatic posting is not triggered.

Could you please confirm whether processing rules for bank statements support the use of non-Latin (e.g., Chinese) characters in memo lines? Additionally, we would appreciate any guidance on how to enable correct processing in such cases.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Claudia

entry.title: Re: Create Maintenance Request App AdaptUI Notif Type Change
entry.content:
Dear @MuratTnrkt,

This is most likely not caused mainly by missing Y1 customizing. In standard Create Maintenance Request (F1511A), SAP states that the app supports selecting the notification type and that Y1 is only the default. SAP also documents a known symptom where the Notification Type field is not available at all and the app creates Y1 by default. Therefore this points more to a difference in the base app / runtime / adaptation-project state between the two projects rather than simply to missing notification type Y1 configuration. Missing Y1 can explain a later save error such as Notification type Y1 not defined but not necessarily why the editable field is absent in Adapt UI. SAP adaptation-project documentation also confirms that project behavior depends on the base app and whether the project is up to date with base app upgrades

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/5d2fbff31efc440b8200fbad95a68dfe.html

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003559950

Summary:

Missing Y1 may explain a save/defaulting problem, but the missing Notification Type field itself is more likely due to an app/runtime/adaptation-project difference

Best Regards,

Dawid

When you create a Blog how long it takes to reviewed and get pubkished?

Thanks

Luis

entry.title: Re: Business Objects Enterprise 4.3 update from SP04 patc...
entry.content:
Hi Andrea,

You will need to make sure Windows Defender real-time protection truly disabled during install. Are exclusions set for:

    • D:\SAP BusinessObjects\
    • D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\ (InstallCache, tmp)
    • %ProgramData%\SAP BusinessObjects\ (if used)
    • %TEMP% and the installer’s extraction directory?

Make sure to stop all BO services (SIA, Tomcat) and verify no BO processes remain in Task Manager.

Disable Windows Search indexing for D:\SAP BusinessObjects\ and subfolders.

Confirm NTFS permissions and ownership:

  • Inherit permissions from D:\SAP BusinessObjects down to InstallData, InstallCache, tmp.
  • Ensure the installer account has Full Control.

Clear stale installer state:

  • Rename D:\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\InstallCache\ to InstallCache.bak and let the installer rebuild cache.
  • Ensure %TEMP% is accessible and not redirected to a restricted path.

Reboot the server before re-attempt to release file locks.

Disable Controlled Folder Access in Defender during the install .

Validate no other agents remain (backup, DLP, EDR remnants). If present, stop services during the upgrade window.

If it still fails:

Use Process Monitor (ProcMon) from Sysinternals during the upgrade, filtered to:

  • Path contains InstallData\tmp
  • Result is ACCESS DENIED

This will show you exactly which process is blocking the file operation.

entry.title: Re: PIX Payment in Brazil – Incoming payment

entry.title: Re: How to query a customer's credit exposure data throug...
entry.content:
Dear a1993-12,

unfortunately, it is not possible to verify without additional insight where the mismatch of the credit exposure values came from.

One possible source of error could be the selection criteria for the CDS View (I_CreditExposureItem).

To solve the problem, please open a customer incident.

Best wishes
Christina

entry.title: Re: SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform 2025 installation Dis...
entry.content:
Hello Agdict,

Even though you're installing to the D drive (119 GB free), the SAP BO installer heavily uses the C: drive during installation for several purposes, and 35 GB on C: is insufficient. Total recommended free on C: ~50–65 GB.

The installation likely fails during the later phases (web tier deployment, Crystal Reports components, or Tomcat deployment) when cumulative TEMP usage peaks.

Thanks!

entry.title: Third-Party Payments with SIPO (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)
entry.content:
This blog post introduces business scenarios and explains how to process payment with SIPO in Czech Republic for SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition.  

SIPO (Soustředěné Inkaso Plateb Obyvatelstva) that is used for direct debit of multiple payments through the Czech Post’s system. It is a national payment aggregation system operated by the Czech Post. This business concept relates to payments through a third party. 

Business Scenarios 

Let’s start with the parties involved in SIPO payments.   

  1. Customers that have SIPO connection number pay on a regular basis  
  2. Service providers that offer services of utilities, multimedia, or internet, send payment requests to SIPO  
  3. Czech Post Office, or the SIPO operator, collects all payment requests, aggregates them into one payment, and distribute paid money by customers to service providers  
  4. Banks that serve as payment channels carry out direct debit from bank accounts 

There are two business scenarios of payments involving SIPO:  

Scenario 1: You, as a service provider, post receivable items. After executing a payment run, you send a SIPO file, as an outgoing file, to the Czech Post for informing the customers to pay.   

Scenario 2: Continuing with scenario 1, you, as a service provider, want to clear the open items by processing incoming SIPO files after your customer completes the payment. 

Prerequisites 

Bank Account Settings   

Since the payments with SIPO order are not directly paid out from the payer's bank account, instead it is paid out from the post office, it is necessary to create a fictive bank account and account ID for the post office.   

Open the Manage Bank Accounts app.   

For your company code, create a fictive Bank Account and Account ID for the Czech Post.

Define Parameters for Paying Out    

Since payment is carried out through the third party (Czech Post),  you need to identify which payee whom the post office pays.    

In the Define Parameters for Foreign Payment Transactions (ID: 101953) configuration to fill the service provider's SIPO number in the Customer number field.  

Define Payment Methods  

Open the Define Payment Methods (ID: 101952) to create a payment method or use the pre-delivered format - CZ_PSTLPAYMENT_SIPO as the payment medium format for Czech SIPO payments.

Define Payment Lot Specifications   

Open the Define Payment Lot Specification activity (ID:107255).   

Select the format type Czech Republic: Postal Payment Item (SIPO file) (CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-ZA) and complete the Bank statement settings section.   
Note: It is not required to configure the Czech Republic: Postal Payment Header (SIPO file) (CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-PZ).

Define Interpretation Rules for Note to Payee    

Open the Define Interpretation Rules for Note to Payee activity (ID: 103949) to assign keywords.

Procedure

Processing SIPO Outgoing Payment Files 

  1. You post contract accounting receivable items.  
  2. Execute a payment run. The payment order then is created.  
  3. During the payment run, the system uses the payment format CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-SIPO to generate the payment file for the Czech Post.  
  4. You then send the payment file to Czech Post.  
    Czech Post informs the customer to conduct the payment.  

Processing SIPO Incoming Payment Files

After the customer pays, the Czech Post sends back the SIPO files.   

The SIPO files contain the following files:  

  • SIPO header file – Includes the total settlement amount that matches the bank statement of the incoming payment  
  • SIPO item file – lists the clearing of open receivable items per customer’s individual SIPO number 

You have to import both files in the Manage Incoming Payment File (3rd-Party Payments) app (F1680).   

  1. Open the Manage Incoming Payment File (3rd-Party Payments) app (F1680). 
    • Select Import and choose Third-party Payment.
    • Select Czech Republic: Postal Payment Header (SIPO file) and upload the SIPO header file.  
    • Repeat the same operation and upload the Czech Republic: Postal Payment Item (SIPO file).  
  2. Open the Process Imported Third-Party Payments app (F9000) to schedule a run. 
    • Select Third-Party Payment: Transfer data as the job template.   
    • In the Parameters, select Czech Republic: Postal Payment Item (SIPO file) (CZ-POSTAL-PAYMENT-ZA) as a format type.   
      Note: When processing the imported file, you only need to process the item files. (Header files are not required.)  
    • Schedule a run and you can see that the payment lot number is created in the log .
      If you select the Post Lot check box and you can directly process the payment order lot without additional operation.  
  3. Run the payment lot and you clear the open items. Use the payment lot and process it in the Process Payment (Order) Lot app and clear open items of the customer. If you did not select the checkbox in the previous step. 

Is this blog post useful to you? Leave your comments below to let us know your thoughts.

entry.title: Turn tax into strategy: Margin steering with SAP PaPM and SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Transfer pricing rarely gets the spotlight.  But it should.

In this partner webinar, we open the doors to a real SAP journey.  A shift from fixed license rates to a margin-based model powered by Transactional Net Margin Method (TNMM).  The goal was clear.  Align tax, profitability, and compliance with the realities of a cloud business.

You will see how SAP built an end-to-end solution using SAP Profitability and Performance Management, SAP Analytics Cloud, and Settlement Management.  Not theory.  Actual execution.

The result.  Better audit readiness.  Less manual work.  A model that scales as the business grows.

The thing is, margin steering is not just a finance exercise.  It gives your customers a way to stay within arm’s length ranges while keeping profitability predictable.

If you work with EPM, xP&A, or tax-driven transformations, this session will give you practical ideas you can apply fast.

Register now and see how strategy meets execution.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/apr/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013760.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPAnalyticsCloud #EPM #xP&A #TransferPricing

entry.title: v 2 oData error
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Kindly check the oData error.

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/thirdparty/jquery",
    "sap/ui/core/date/UI5Date",
    "sap/ui/core/util/MockServer",
    "sap/base/Log"
], (jQuery, UI5Date, MockServer, Log) => {
    "use strict";

    return {
        /**
         * Initializes the mock server.
         * You can configure the delay with the URL parameter "serverDelay".
         * The local mock data in this folder is returned instead of the real data for testing.
         * @Public
         */
        init() {
            // create
            const oMockServer = new MockServer({rootUri: "/"});

            oMockServer.simulate("../localService/metadata.xml", {
                sMockdataBaseUrl: "../localService/mockdata",
                bGenerateMissingMockData: true
            });
            // handling mocking a function import call step
            const aRequests = oMockServer.getRequests();
            aRequests.push({
                method: "GET",
                path: new RegExp("FindUpcomingMeetups(.*)"),
                response: (oXhr) => {
                    Log.debug("Incoming request for FindUpcomingMeetups");
                    const oToday = UI5Date.getInstance();
                    oToday.setHours(0); // or oToday.toUTCString(0) due to timezone differences
                    oToday.setMinutes(0);
                    oToday.setSeconds(0);
                    // the mock server only works with sap.jQuery.ajax and async: false. But the request does not
                    // actually go to a server, so this does not block the main thread.
                    jQuery.ajax({
                        url: `/Meetups?$filter=EventDate ge datetime'${oToday.toISOString()}'`,
                        dataType : 'json',
                        async: false,
                        success : (oData) => {
                            oXhr.respondJSON(200, {}, JSON.stringify(oData));
                        }
                    });
                    return true;
                }
            });
            oMockServer.setRequests(aRequests);

            // handling custom URL parameter step
            const fnCustom = (oEvent) => {
                const oXhr = oEvent.getParameter("oXhr");
                if (oXhr?.url.includes("first")) {
                    oEvent.getParameter("oFilteredData").results.splice(3, 100);
                }
            };
            oMockServer.attachAfter("GET", fnCustom, "Meetups");


            // start
            oMockServer.start();

            Log.info("Running the app with mock data");
        }
    };
});

Thanks

Kaushik

entry.title: Re: Upload Excel using SAP RAP Only
entry.content: If you want to save the data in a standard way, for example: material loading (without clean core), it would consume a BAPI, and where would you use it? SAP API? Another question: with a very high volume of data, is this application on RAP at risk of timeout?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Action Box linking F...
entry.content:
Dear user,

the "Manage Complaints Against Supplier" refers to a new Fiori object that has been introduced (see here) with relevant data of defective materials can be recorded in the new Complaint against Supplier, and subsequently returned to the supplier by automatically creating a returns purchase order from the Complaint. The new Fiori object has no association with the Quality Notification and is to be considered as a modern Fiori-based alternative to using the Quality Notification for the notification type 'Q2'. 

Warm regards
Varun

entry.title: app for loading materials while respecting clean core principles
entry.content:
I want to create an application that loads materials in Excel format and inserts the data according to the standard. Previously, I would have used a BAPI, but from what I understand, this wouldn't be compatible with the SAP core. What would be the correct approach: RAP -> SAP API? Considering that using only RAP could cause time limit issues for very large volumes?

S4 -> ON-PREMISE 2021

entry.title: Re: IF YOU HAD TO RESTART YOUR SAP CAREER TODAY -- CAPM or UI5?

entry.title: Re: Business Objects Enterprise 4.3 update from SP04 patc...
entry.content: Dear Mauricio First of all, thank you very much for your suggestions, I appreciate them very much also because I'm now groping in the dark. In my failed upgrade attempts, I followed some of your advice. I have, however, a doubt. You wrote: Make sure to stop all BO services (SIA, Tomcat) and verify no BO processes remain in Task Manager. How is this possible? In the dialog phase of the upgrade, the credentials are asked and a connection attempt is made. If the SIA is not active, how can the dialog phase continue? Thank you again very much.

entry.title: BPC Distribution Function: 'New Template' and 'Open Template' Buttons Greyed Out
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

As shown below, the 'New Template' and 'Open Template' buttons are greyed out on the Distribution Wizard in BPC. Is there a way to make these buttons accessible without adjusting the task profile?

 SAP Business Planning and Consolidation, version for SAP NetWeaver SAP Business Planning and Consolidation, version for SAP BW/4HANA SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Cheques Encashment date field is missing in FBL1N table
entry.content:
In ECC, the Cheque Encashment Date field was available in classic line item reports like FBL1N because those reports pulled directly from the classic tables (BSIK/BSID/BSEG) and displayed many payment-related fields.

The Cheque Encashment Date field is not part of the standard field catalog in FBL1N in S/4HANA. Instead, payment-related details (including cheque information) are accessed through Payment Information reports or via the Universal Journal (ACDOCA) and related CDS views.

So what you can do is use Fiori apps like Supplier Line Items or Supplier Balances which are based on CDS views. These apps expose payment information differently. If the business requires the Cheque Encashment Date in FBL1N or equivalent, you can extend the CDS view behind the report to include the field from the payment tables.

entry.title: Re: odataVersion 4.01 prevents app from being imported into Work Zone HTML5 Apps
entry.content:
I'm surprised this is not a bigger issue since I just recently used the fiori generator and it seems like OData 4.01 is now the default.

As a test, I tried this with a ui5 app I have connected to an OData 4.01 RAP service. Before deploying the app, I set the manifest.json to 4.0. This obviously caused an error when connecting to the RAP service, "unsupported OData version". So I implemented a very hacky monkey patch to switch the version back to 4.01 at runtime.

Sure enough, it is only the import that does not work - once you can get it to be recognized by Build Work Zone it doesn't matter what version it is, the app runs fine.

I'm not planning on really using this in the app, I just wanted to verify that it is only the deployment that fails. In the end my solution was not so interesting, I just reverted the backend service to use 4.0. I hope the Build Work Zone team will address this quickly since there must be a lot of users with apps using 4.01 now who are experiencing issues with Build Work Zone thanks to the generator.

P.S. It is not only Build Work Zone that does not support version 4.01 - the UI5 Language Assistant also seems to have an issue. When my localService folder's metadata.xml has version 4.01 set, all the XML views lose their code completion in VS Code. Setting it back to 4.0 fixes the issue.

entry.title: How to prevent clearing Vendor Payment with another Vendor in SAP S4 hana
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to prevent the clearing of invoices from one vendor against another vendor,  within the Fiori tile Clear outgoing payments . How can this be configured?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: SAP BDC - Semantic onboarding - Calculation Views Import
entry.content:
Dear all!

I understood that it will (or may even already be possible) to "import" CV definitions in SAP HANA Cloud with consumption in SAP BDC.

The 2 modes are not really clear for me. 

I read that the Flat view option is not working for Dimension view. So, only Cube views can be used there ?

Does the Construction Kit option mean that only cube views can be imported and reused or does it cover also Dimension views ? 

Also, when using this approach, is the tool wise enough to retrieve all the underlying views that are used ? For example, if I have a cube view reading another dimension view which is a complex view joining and creating unions and rankings of several other view, can we use this "last view" and then all the underlying views will be analysed to be recreated as well ?

 

PY

entry.title: From Spreadsheets to Strategic Planning: Smarter Program Budgeting Starts with SAC
entry.content:
For years, Marketing Operations at SAP faced the same challenges every year: navigating a fast-paced budgeting cycle using Excel spreadsheets. Data lived in silos, collaboration was difficult, and leadership lacked real-time visibility into how investment decisions would impact the business. Despite the team’s best efforts, the process wasn’t good enough for the speed and precision marketing requires these days. 

A Clear Need for Transformation 

The marketing budgeting process needs a single source of truth- one centralized place where marketing and financial data can come together. Offline spreadsheets have become very complex, are slow to update, not easy to interpret and can have errors due to manual updates. Teams need transparency and a tool that eliminates manual effort to consolidate datasets. 

Most importantly, there is a need to make quick decisions during planning calls, not hours or days later after analysing the spreadsheets 

Building a Smarter, Faster Planning Environment with SAC 

Leveraging the existing SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), Marketing Operations and CPIT teams joined to design a fully integrated planning module built directly in SAC. 

Using learnings from previous planning cycles, the team recreated the functionality of legacy spreadsheets—then enhanced it using SAC’s capabilities: 

  • Live planning data from core systems, all centralized inside SAC 
  • Data actions, fluid data entry, and currency conversion  
  • Multiple versions to model different scenarios and compare changes  
  • Automated master data sourcing, reducing maintenance and ensuring consistency 
  • A dashboard layer that visualizes changes and tells the story behind the numbers 
  • A data architecture prepared for downstream integrations into CRM and ISP 

The result is a collaborative, real-time planning experience, removing the need to email spreadsheets back and forth or wait for someone to “update the spreadsheet.” 

The Results: Real-Time Planning With Real Impact 

The impact of moving to SAC Planning was immediate: 

  • 94% faster calculations compared to offline spreadsheets 
  • Instant scenario modeling with real-time feedback for leadership 
  • Over 41 marketing attributes blended with financial data for deeper insights 
  • ~480 parameters simplified into three strategic investment levers 
  • Automated budget cascades across 10 organizations, five solution areas, and 8 Marketing Mix types 
  • 100% adoption of SAP running SAP, improving transparency and trust 
  • Elimination of manual imports into visualization tools 

What previously required hours of manual adjustments and spreadsheet now happens in minutes. Leaders can explore scenarios live, evaluate impacts, and align decisions without waiting. 

As M&S CCO Tom Lavin put it: 

“We used our technology to streamline the modeling efforts and make key decisions in real time.” 

This wasn’t just process improvement—it was a full transformation of how Marketing plans investments and responds to change. 

Laying the Foundation for the Future 

With SAC Planning in place, Marketing now has the agility and governance needed to continuously evolve. The new tool brings clarity, consistency, and confidence to planning decisions, setting the stage for broader SAC adoption across the entire Marketing Program Budget Planning process. 

When we start using SAP technology the way it’s meant to be used, we unlock not just efficiency—but smarter, more strategic decision-making.

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition 2025 FPS1
entry.content: Is SAP Geographical Enablement Framework (GEF) a separate add-on?

entry.title: Re: Credit management related issues - update group chang...
entry.content:
Dear Blake45,

First, I’d like to confirm that reassigning the  update group from 000012 to 000015 was the correct approach for this situation.

Regarding your questions:

1) Removing the reassigned SO value after the change of the update group:

To solve this problem, you have to rebuild the commitments after changing the update group. You can do this in the Rebuild Credit Management Data (F4234) app. Here's a link to the documentation of the job template: Rebuild Credit Exposure

2) Error after releasing the documented credit decision

For this type of issue, please open a customer incident so our support team can investigate further.

Best wishes
Christina

entry.title: Re: Week 2 – Building AI Agents with CrewAI and Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Week-2 Challenge completed. 

User: GE303408

entry.title: Re: From Legacy ERP to SAP S/4HANA: Why Quality Governance is the Secret Sauce
entry.content: Spot on, @Corrie ! 'Get it clean, then keep it clean' is the ultimate golden rule. In my experience with complex S/4HANA migrations, technical success is often hollow if data integrity isn't governed from day one. It truly is the difference between a system that just 'runs' and a system that actually 'delivers business value'. Thanks for sharing this expert perspective!

entry.title: Re: MuleSoft vs SAP Integration Suite: Which One Fits Your Enterprise?
entry.content:
SAP CPI does not have currently a solution for individual message processing for Event Mesh messages. Which is a huge miss! ( Only for JMS, but the User Experience for that JMS is very low.  )

SAP CPI does not have a built-in resend functionality, what I assume MuleSoft has.  CPI does not persist the messages only with additional development effort.  This makes CPI a half-ready product in my opinion, because the error handling is just simply not there yet.

entry.title: Re: Basic Credit Management (BD6): Exlcude a payment term...
entry.content:
Dear Simon,

At the moment, it isn’t possible to exclude a specific payment term from the credit limit calculation.

If you would like to request a change to the system logic and this requirement is critical for your business, please feel free to submit a development request. 

SAP actively encourages customers to help shape future product enhancements. Your feedback is reviewed by our product owners and can directly influence upcoming developments. More information about the SAP Customer Influence program is available here:

  • http://influence.sap.com 
  • Would you like to shape & influence future releases of SAP Products? (SAP Note)

Best wishes,
Christina

entry.title: The function module CONVERT_BDCMSGCOLL_TO_BAPIRET2 does not exist in the system S/4 release 2023
entry.content:
Hi

We have upgraded to HANA 2023 and we had used the function module CONVERT_BDCMSGCOLL_TO_BAPIRET2 in some of our programs.  As per SAP note 3411865, The function module CONVERT_BDCMSGCOLL_TO_BAPIRET2 does not exist in the system S/4 release 2023. We have checked for any replacement FM but didn't find any. This note is also not implementable. Kindly provide an alternate FM as we need this functionality for some of our developments

 

Regards

Vijay

entry.title: Re: Uploading bulk manual test cases in test preparation app
entry.content:
Hello, 

Mass upload of Test case is possible, please find https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/new-mass-upload-of-test-cases-in-sap-cloud-alm/ba-p/14090247 for more details.

Mass update is currently not possible.

Thanks,

Aj

entry.title: Color Configuration for Latest Home Page Experience
entry.content:
Hello, 

I'm trying to configure the Latest Home Page for our SuccessFactors instance and I want to update the (currently translucent) box with my greeting and Quick Links in it to an opaque navy blue (see screenshot below). I went to "Manage Themes" and specifically clicked the theme related to our Landing Page, but found: 

  1. This appears to be showing the previous preview of the Home Page (despite "Latest" being selected in Preview Styling) 
  2. The Greeting block is already displaying the navy blue that I want

Any help, resources, or direction you can help point me in would be appreciated!

Screenshot of the Latest Home Page I am trying to configure:

Screenshot of the Manage Themes page where I'd hoped this was where I could make the update:

Thank you!
Geena

entry.title: Cannot add new entries in Define attributes of system message to block when No info record exist
entry.content:
Hello

I'm trying to enable this system message  136 application area 06 to get a blocking message when no info record is found at the purchase order level.

Apparently we cannot add new entries in SAP S4HANA public cloud for Define attributes of system message, Is there any work around available ?

thanks in advance

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Production Planning and Manufacturing Certification – course overview and 2026 dates
entry.content:
To make your path to certification clear and structured, we’ve organized the learning journey into three parts: an optional basics overview, mandatory modules for certification preparation, and an additional deep-dive course. We’re also offering bundled tracks with fixed dates.

Basics overview (optional, for beginners)

  • S42010 – Exploring Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Production Planning
  • S42020 – Exploring Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Discrete Shopfloor Control
  • S42025 – Exploring Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Process Shopfloor Control

Mandatory trainings for certification preparation

  • S4210 – Basic Data for Manufacturing and Product Management in SAP S/4HANA
  • S4222 – Production Planning in SAP S/4HANA – Fiori-based
  • S4225 – SAP S/4HANA Production Orders

Additional recommended training (deep dive)

  • S4224 – Production Planning with SAP S/4HANA PP/DS

Recommended learning sequence

  • Optional foundation: S42010/S42020/S42025 (overview)
  • Mandatory modules in this order: S4210 → S4222 → S4225
  • Optional afterwards: S4224 (PP/DS) for advanced planning

Tracks and dates 2026

  • Track 1 (May/June 2026)

    • S4210: May 18–22, 2026 (Walldorf or SAP Live Class, SLC)
    • S4222: May 27–29, 2026 (SAP Live Class, SLC)
    • S4225: June 15–19, 2026 (Walldorf or SAP Live Class, SLC)

  • Track 2 (September 2026)

    • S4210: September 7–11, 2026 (Walldorf or SAP Live Class, SLC)
    • S4222: September 14–16, 2026 (SAP Live Class, SLC)
    • S4225: October 05–09, 2026 (Hamburg or SAP Live Class, SLC)

  • Track 3 (December 2026)

    • S4210: November 30–December 4, 2026 (Walldorf or SAP Live Class, SLC)
    • S4225: December 7–11, 2026 (Walldorf or SAP Live Class, SLC)
    • S4222: December 14–16, 2026 (SAP Live Class, SLC)

Notes for exam preparation

The three mandatory modules cover the core topics assessed in the SAP S/4HANA Production Planning and Manufacturing certification. Plan the exam after completing all mandatory modules and allow 1–2 weeks for review and hands-on practice. The basics overview is optional but helps newcomers get up to speed with processes and terminology. Formats: on-site in Walldorf, Hamburg or virtual, as indicated above.

entry.title: Re: Absence Counting Rules 2.0 in Time Management - 2H 2023
entry.content:
Hi @PriyankaAgarwal 

First of all, great blog and thank you for sharing such valuable insights.

We have an extremely flexible workforce where employees frequently work based on demand. As a result, we are using Temporary Work Schedules for these scenarios. From your explanation, it is clear that we can either:

  1. Use FTE/Working Hours per Week, and/or
  2. Use Employee Time: Number of Hours in the Absence Counting Rule parameters.

We opted for the second approach, as it better fits our current landscape.

While this works as expected for single-day absences, we are experiencing issues with multi-day absences. Specifically, when an employee has reduced hours on certain days (which can vary frequently), those days should count as 0.5 instead of 1.

For example, if an employee requests a full week of absence covering five working days, and two of those days have reduced hours, the system should count 4 days instead of 5.

Could you please advise which approach you would recommend in this scenario?

Additionally, I understand that there were plans to include Work Schedule as a parameter in the Absence Counting Rule. Is this enhancement already planned or available in the roadmap?

Thank you once again for your support.

Cheers,
Pedro

entry.title: Re: Issue with Bank Statement Processing Rules for Bank Fees
entry.content:
Dear @Claudia234,

In Public Cloud using Memo Line as a condition in bank statement processing rules is supported so the rule design itself is valid. However, SAP documents that interpretation algorithm 001 (Standard Algorithm) processes only alphanumeric characters and does not handle special-character scenarios. Based on this documented standard limitation, automatic posting should not be considered reliable when the memo line contains non-alphanumeric or special characters, including cases where the memo line contains Chinese text even if the same rule works for Latin text such as BANK CHARGES

The SAP-supported approach is to avoid relying on standard algorithm 001 for this case and instead use processing instruction 011 which SAP explicitly states can be used in Reprocess Bank Statement Items or through processing rules

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3555197

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/4865ed7d26304aa1b011db7a52131bea.html

In addition, it is important to verify that the Chinese text is imported into SAP exactly as received from the bank. SAP also documents that if the bank statement uses a structured memo line in field :86:, the correct format handling is required, for example MT940_STRUCTURED instead of the standard MT940 mapping

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/5481c611480e4c23bf49e3af651d8e4d.html

Summary:

The issue is most likely a standard interpretation limitation, not a problem with the posting rule target account. For memo text containing non-alphanumeric or special characters, including Chinese text, the recommended SAP-supported solution is to first verify that the text is imported into SAP as expected and that the correct statement format is used and then use processing instruction 011 instead of relying on standard memo-line matching with algorithm 001

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: How I Connected Claude AI to My SAP ABAP System Using MCP — A Complete Windows Guide
entry.content:
@Ahmed_Mosbah , You nailed it well ! This was one of the point of discussion with my friend just few days back and i see it is happening now. Thanks again for sharing it. 

Regards,

Sandeep

entry.title: SAC Live Connection to S/4HANA Private Cloud via CORS
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community, I'm currently connecting SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition using the CORS Direct method for a Live Data Connection. I'm running into two issues that I haven't been able to fully resolve, and I'd appreciate any guidance from the community.

My current setup

  • SAP Analytics Cloud 
  • SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition 
  • Live Data Connection configured using CORS Direct method
  • Authentication method: User Name and Password (Basic Auth)

Issue 1 - Credentials required every time I open a story

Every time I open a Story that uses a Live model connected to S/4HANA, I get prompted to enter my S/4HANA username and password before the data loads.

My current workaround: I open a Fiori launchpad session in a separate browser tab first so that the S/4HANA session cookie exists in the browser, and then the data loads in SAC without the popup.

This is not a sustainable solution for end users. Is this related to the SSL certificate on the S/4HANA side? Or is this purely a missing SSO configuration?

Issue 2 - SAC mobile app: stories load with no data (design only)

On the SAC mobile app (iOS/Android) (or the browser same issue ), I can log into SAC successfully, I can see my stories and the design renders correctly, but no data is loaded. The charts and tables are empty.

I tried opening a Fiori session in the mobile browser first (same workaround as desktop), but it doesn't help, data still doesn't appear in the app.

My understanding is that CORS relies on browser-level cookie sharing, which native mobile apps don't support in the same way. Is this correct? Is CORS fundamentally incompatible with the SAC mobile app/ browser for Live Connections?

 

SO my question for the community : 

- How eliminate the S/4HANA credential popup everytime ??

- Is there a recommended architecture that handles both desktop and mobile in a Private Cloud / On-Premise-like context?

entry.title: Re: SAP Composable Storefront – ASM - data isolation true - B2B site
entry.content:
Hi @balaji_k1,

I add that we upgrade to 221121.9, and in log I see this error

Translating exception [org.springframework.security.authentication.InsufficientAuthenticationException]: Full authentication is required to access this resource

 

Stefania

entry.title: SAC - Export incomplete without the page Initialization
entry.content:
Hello Team,

In SAC, the issue I encountered was that I had written initialization scripts across 14 pages of the story. However, as the client starts exporting the story without navigating through all the pages, the export remains incomplete. As a result, the exported file contained partially loaded data.

So I tried 1st initializing all the pages and then exporting, but this is taking way longer than expected,

Thanks in advance for your suggestions/workarounds.

entry.title: Re: ODBC failure on basic call
entry.content:
This seems to only impact SQL_C_WCHAR and not SQL_C_CHAR.

Setting the buffer size to an even number appears to resolve this.  The sample code uses a buffer of 1025 but if it is 1026, there isn't an error.  My guess is that ODBC expects the wide character to be a multiple of 2.   

I think this is a driver manager issue rather than a driver behaviour.

EDIT: My previous note related to the +1 is incorrect.

entry.title: Re: The function module CONVERT_BDCMSGCOLL_TO_BAPIRET2 does not exist in the system S/4 release 2023
entry.content: Wrap the logic in a Z function module and adapt the artifacts that were calling that function module.

entry.title: Re: RBP Permissions have been affected with the new peopl...
entry.content: Thankyou for the response. We did compare with the old people profile Employee views Permission Report and noticed that only 5 of the RBP roles have been updated with the Compensation permission under People Profile when we selected RBP for Compensation category whereas remaining were not changed. So what is the logic behind updating only few of them with the appropriate permissions. Is it an expected behavior?

entry.title: Interview Assessment Pending notification - SF RECRUITING
entry.content:
I created and enabled an email template to cover pending interview assessments.

Then enabled the recruiting mail trigger for the "Interview Assessments Outstanding" with this condition: "If an interview assessment form has been incomplete for 1 day following the interview date, send an email every 1 day. If no interview date is specified, this email does not trigger. Send a maximum of 3 reminder emails."

---

However, the notifications are not being sent.

After reading some SAP KBAs, I have done the following:

  • Checked that the provisioning job isn't failing. It runs completely.
  • Used reporting that the pending interview assessments are not more than 32,000 to ensure they are not stopping the mails from coming through.
  • Ensure that 'Enable Stack Ranker-style Interview Assessments' is ticked.

----

In addition, with the 1H2026 Release for the new applicant management experience, using the nudge button in the feedback pending column, the email notification triggers.

Kindly advise what else I can do for the email trigger to work as expected without clicking the nudge button.

Thank You!

entry.title: Re: Frequently Asked Questions on Sales Quote in SAP Sales Cloud V2
entry.content:
Hi  Ashwin,

GAP: ¿What happens with the quote in S4 if you cancel the quote in C4C? This change is not sent to S4, so documents are missaligned!

GAP: If we create the ERP Order from the C4C Quote, how can we set the reason for the status in the Quote? This field is not editable when the quote is completed...

GAP: If we set the quote as won because we don't want to create the sales order yet, the "Create ERP Order" button is disabled. So we can't create the ERP Order from C4C as expected...
